First time ever dealing with this process Adam took his time breaking everything down to me I hit him with back to back questions and he took his time with me throughout the whole process calls, texts emails he is on point with everything on top of a great lawyer his team is absolutely incredible I emailed his assistant after hours and bam within an hour I got a response I was surprised if you want a law firm that will fight for you and give it there all go with Adam you won’t regret it!!

A waste of time lawyer that is only out for making thousands of dollars and if not he will just toss you to the side and ignore you.  He doesn't respond to texts, calls, or emails for days, weeks, and even months at a time.  He ignores questions that you may have.  He states he is to busy to even to reply to a yes or no question in a text.  Avoid this man and find a more experienced lawyer that will follow through with his words. He loses the case then places blame on the victim of assault.  He will also get you by getting paid for your case that was lost even if it is won by a new lawyer so that you end up with nothing and he pockets the money that is deserved to go the victim.  Avoid this man at all costs, he is only about the money and not the victim.  True story from Kentucky.

KVP represented a HOA that was not even registered with the state of Florida. They did zero due diligence and illegally forced me to engage in mediation with an entity that was not legitimate. I have all the documents detailing the events. Needless to say it was a serious waste of everyone’s time but they got their money. Stay far away from these clowns.

I wanted to share what a great experience it was to work with both Attorney James "Jim" Pickens and his very capable Real Estate Paralegal & Assistant, Joanne Marchese, at KVP LAW PL...

I was asked to consider a partition suit for a client of their firm and I was very impressed with the professionalism that Mr. Pickens and his staff exhibited throughout the entire 16 month process, with court hearings and what not, that I was involved with whilst working towards selling the subject and settling the matter...

These folks truly care about their client and will certainly watch over them, in every aspect of their process to attain their goals...

Being involved for over 38 years in real estate transactions throughout my work careers, I would happily recommend these folks to anyone looking to resolve any of their legal matters and/or utilize their general real estate services as a closing agent. The team there delivers a very clean and professional presentation package leaving the client assured that no stone was left unturned...

Keep up the great work folks and see you again soon!

reviews.text: We were lied to at closing. We were lied to by our attorney and the Closing agent that works at the office in the same building.  We were promised a parcel release and was told it will be given to us in a couple of days and was already agreed upon.  At no point did they say there would be stipulations in the agreement just that it was agreed that we would receive the parcel release agreement within a few days after signing the title.    The papers were pushed across the table to us and we were advised to sign by our  attorney and the closing agent.    I want to be very clear the closing agent said that she was there when they agreed to give us the parcel release.   She and oue attorney reassured us of this.    Even though another attorney that does not litigate ...advised us to get the parcel release and not to sign unless we do.  We explain that to our attorney at the closing table and they still pushed it across for us to sign saying that we already had an agreement and we would have it in hand in a few days.     We explained to our attorney that another attorney that they know very well advised us not to sign unless we had the parcel release .   That same attorney also told me that James was a great attorney and we could trust him.   So we did and here we are.  It has now put me and my family in a financial situation that is terrible . Not to mention the strain it has put on us mentally.   We are at risk losing our dreams because we were left uninformed misinformed and directly lied to.  I would never trust this Firm or the Closing Title Company that works with this firm in the same building .    Do not believe a word they say at closing unless you have it in writing.   I cannot say it enough do not trust this Firm .

I cannot recommend Matt Thompson highly enough. From the moment we first met, it was clear that he was not only incredibly intelligent but also deeply committed to his clients’ success. Together, we set clear goals for my case, and I was consistently impressed by how focused and aligned he stayed with those objectives throughout the entire process.

Matt possesses a rare balance of traits that make him an exceptional defense lawyer. He is aggressive when fighting for his clients’ rights but does so with integrity and fairness. His kindness and understanding were evident during our conversations—he truly listened to my concerns and ensured I always felt supported and informed. At the same time, his relentless determination and sharp strategic thinking left no stone unturned in my defense.

Honesty is another defining quality of Matt. He was straightforward about the challenges we faced and realistic in setting expectations while remaining optimistic and confident, but only of what was capable or practical. This transparency built an immense amount of trust, which proved invaluable when navigating such a stressful and uncertain situation.

If you’re looking for a lawyer who is not only a skilled advocate, relentless fighter, but also someone who genuinely cares about your well-being, I wholeheartedly recommend Matt Thompson. A+++

Matt is a down to earth Attorney with your best interest in mind. He speaks to you directly and not like other firms where he is just the owner and or face of the company. He gives you a cell number that he will answer texts and calls to for any confusion or simple questions. My case ended up being dismissed before the pre trial hearing. Thanks Matt!

I was looking forward to consulting with Mr. Shapiro regarding my father's assets, will, and maybe the creation of a potential trust as his Power of Attorney since he's recently been placed in Hospice/memory care. Mr. Shapiro has great reviews here and I felt this would be a good place to start. Unfortunately, the young lady who answered the call stated she wasn't sure that the attorney would want to speak with me since my father's POA was created by an attorney out of state years ago. I explained that my father is a current resident of Florida, as am I, and I was looking to consult with an attorney to ensure my father's assets were protected and to see what, if anything, I would need to do as his POA. I've never dealt with anything like this and need guidance. She stated that she'd need to talk with the attorney to see if he'd even want to meet with me. I'll be calling other attorneys in the area to see if they can help our family.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am the attorney to whom this review appears to be directed. I am sorry to hear that your initial conversation with a member of our staff was not a positive experience. I happened to overhear the call, and my understanding is that our employee was simply trying to explain that she wanted to speak with me first before scheduling an appointment.
Without getting into any details that could be confidential, there was some concern that the request you described might not be feasible. She was attempting to confirm whether we could actually assist before asking you to come in for a consultation, incur a fee, or spend time on a meeting that might not be productive. Her intent was to avoid wasting your time, not to be dismissive or disrespectful. There are many capable attorneys in the area, and I am confident you will be able to find someone who can provide the assistance you need. reviews.ownerResponse:
While I respect a client’s perspective on an issue of opinion, this post does not accurately nor truthfully reflect the reality of the representation for this client. Without disclosing confidential information or compromising this client’s privacy, I can say that our firm utilizes a standard retainer agreement, which is explicit in its explanations regarding initial and supplemental retainers. The same language appears in every agreement and is emphasized. In addition to the written agreement, we verbally explain our billing policies to ensure clarity. The agreement further notes that supplemental retainers may be necessary and are expected to be provided timely upon request. Clients may request numerous documents to be prepared or other work to be done depending on changes in their immediate circumstances. At times, clients may change their minds regarding filing the same or their overall approach to their case. We have a strong commitment and approach to acting in our client’s best interests while also balancing that with changed requests from a client. However, continued changes results in more work.

I firmly believe that the reviewer received strong legal representation and that each request was accommodated immediately to the fullest extent possible under the circumstances. Details of Ms. Brault’s circumstances would not be appropriate to discuss in a public forum. However, the legal profession is just that – a profession. Our retainer agreements explicitly state the terms, and financial requests, regarding the course of our representation. As in any contract, if that is unable to be fulfilled on either end, the representation must conclude.

This response is provided by the attorney who represented the reviewer. While I respect every client’s perspective, I do not believe this post accurately reflects the events.

Without disclosing confidential information or compromising the former client’s privacy, I can clarify that our firm uses a standard retainer agreement, which explicitly states: “I agree to pay you as retainer for your professional services a minimum sum of $______, which is a non-refundable retainer.” This language appears in every agreement, emphasized in bold and underlined. In addition to the written agreement, we also verbally explain our billing policies to ensure clarity. The agreement further notes that the retainer covers only a portion of the total cost of legal services.

Beyond providing clear explanations of our billing practices, both in writing and verbally, I am unsure what more could have been done to address any misunderstanding. I firmly believe that the reviewer received strong legal representation and that their interests were advocated for to the fullest extent possible under the circumstances—details of which would not be appropriate to discuss in a public forum.

All unused funds beyond the non-refundable retainer have been refunded, and our firm wishes this individual the best moving forward.

reviews.text: Astronomical $5,000 retainer fee. Also requests thousands more for a simple divorce with no property or assets. I would not recommend this attorney because he simply does not truly care about you, your family or situation, he just wants to empty your pocket to fill his.
reviews.ownerResponse: All clients are provided and sign fee agreements which detail the fees they will be charged and the services for which they will be billed. Our initial payment and hourly rates are average for the community and monthly invoices are submitted for clients as their case progresses to provide regular updates of time spent on their cases. Many factors affect the total time spent on a case including the willingness of both parties to try to resolve their issues or to draw lines in the sand. As I recall this particular case settled at mediation on terms the client found acceptable. One issue was left for a hearing before the court and the judge had to rule on that issue. The time incurred beyond the initial hours were charged off when it became apparent the cost of the additional fees would present a hardship. Only two years after conclusion of the case did any concern get raised and then only in this online review rather than with my office. Our goal is to address and try to resole any issues raised by clients but it can't be done if not brought to our attention.

My journey with Sam Masters began after a car accident that occurred over two years ago. At that time, I had initially intended to handle my dealings with the insurance company on my own. However, as the gravity of my injuries became apparent, I realized I needed legal assistance. My initial choice led me to a large law firm with prominent billboards around town, assuming that their visibility equated to quality service. Unfortunately, I found myself just another faceless client with little access to an attorney.

My attempts to seek clarification on their contract went unanswered, which prompted me to search for an attorney who genuinely cared about my case. After reading numerous reviews, I came across Sam Masters.

When I initially reached out to Sam Masters' office, I was informed that he was away at a conference. However, to my surprise, he personally returned my call within a few hours, taking the time to discuss my case for over 30 minutes. A few days later, during our in-person meeting, he patiently answered all my questions and concerns, assuring me of his continued availability for any future inquiries.

Over the course of two years, Sam Masters and his paralegal, Kerri, consistently lived up to their promise of accessibility. They were responsive and kept me informed about the progress of my case.

Working with Sam Masters was a pleasure. He secured a settlement that far exceeded my initial expectations, demonstrating his unwavering commitment to achieving the best possible outcome. Sam Masters genuinely cares about his clients and goes above and beyond to ensure their well-being and the success of their cases.

If you are in need of a personal injury attorney in Volusia County, Sam Masters is undoubtedly the best choice.

reviews.text:
I was referred to this office by the The Florida Bar for a potential consultation. When I contacted the office to request an appointment with an attorney, the senior staff member who answered the call dismissed my request and did not allow me the opportunity to speak with or schedule a consultation with an attorney, despite my express request to do so.

Additionally, during the conversation, she stated that I would have to pay $5,000, in a manner that I perceived as dismissive and discourteous. I felt that my inquiry was not handled professionally and that I was not afforded a fair opportunity to discuss my matter with counsel.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Let's be clear you are not a client and made an inquiry and because you did not like our honest assessment to your inquiry. You thought it was best to write a bad review. Our staff is small, highly trained, and dedicated to treating every caller with professional empathy. We believe in a transparent approach to legal representation, which begins the moment you call. The team consults with the caller for free to determine the viability of your case. We will tell you upfront if a case is not worth pursuing. In addition, if we believe legal fees will outweigh your potential outcome, we will advise you not to move forward. We always encourage callers to seek a second opinion if they wish to explore other options.

Transparency in Pricing: While the initial screening is free, our legal services are premium. Retainers: Start at $5,000, though most are higher based on complexity. All hourly rates and requirements are discussed up front with every caller.

Our Professional Standards
My staff is my second family. They are kind, professional, and here to help. To maintain a healthy work environment, we have a zero-tolerance policy for mistreatment. Our team is authorized to end any call where they are being treated rudely. We believe a successful attorney-client relationship is built on mutual respect. Therefore, in conclusion we are not a good fit. We wish you the best in your search for counsel.

reviews.text: I reached out to this firm for their advertised free 30-minute consultation to briefly outline the facts of my case and understand my legal options. I was informed that the attorney would not review any facts of my case or discuss potential legal options during that time. Based on that response, I decided this was not the right firm for my needs. That left me unclear as to the purpose or value of the consultation. For those seeking meaningful initial guidance, it may be worth confirming in advance what the consultation actually includes.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Hickey as a follow-up to my calling you to inquire if this was a misunderstanding or a training opportunity for my intake staff. Thank you for speaking with me. Let's be clear you are not a client and you made an initial inquiry. As I mentioned yes we do offer free consultations for the type of cases we would accept. Yes we do provide initial thoughts and advice within the limited 30 minute time frame, but at this point it can only be based on what the caller provides as the caller is not a client yet. If the caller becomes a client we would then perform a deep dive into the case. Your consultation will include:
• Up to 30 minutes with an attorney.
• Discussion of general legal concepts relevant to your issue.
• Assessment of whether your issue has a potential legal remedy.
• Initial evaluation of the potential viability of your case.
• Determination of whether our firm can accept your case or if a referral to another attorney is appropriate.
• Recommended steps our firm would take if a viable case to address your issue.
• Estimated cost and timeline for resolution.

Note: Some issues may be resolved within the 30-minute consultation, resulting in no further action.
Here it may be both a case of misunderstanding and a training opportunity for the intake employee who is new. We strive to provide the most professional support when someone calls so I hope this helps and thank you again for speaking with me. We wish you the best.

reviews.text: When they say they're going to call you back they don't and then when you write I Google review they come back with a ridiculous response that makes no sense.
reviews.ownerResponse: Let's be clear. You are not a client. You requested a free consultation and we scheduled a free consultation. We sent you an email confirming the consultation and what to expect during the consultation. We sent email reminders of the consultation. We called at the scheduled day and time and you did not answer (no show). We followed up with an email outlining our no show policy. You called again and we scheduled a 2nd consultation. We sent an email confirming the 2nd consultation and sent reminder emails of that 2nd consultation. We called and again at the scheduled day and time and you did not answer, 2nd no show. At this point this is not a good fit therefore we ask that you contact another attorney. We wish you the best.

reviews.text: Called to schedule a consultation. I spoke to a Rosemary on the phone and received an email to confirm that consultation. The day of the consultation I waited on a phone call after 30 minutes of waiting I called them and they said I didn't answer the phone. They repeated back a number and they had the wrong number. Rosemary said that they couldn't do the consultation today and rescheduled me. The second time I never received a phone call and 12 minutes after not receiving the phone call I called them. Rosemary said they tried calling and, there was nothing they could do, they would not offer the consultation any longer.
reviews.ownerResponse: To respect the time of our licensed attorneys and ensure the highest level of service for all clients, we have established a firm consultation policy. Upon scheduling, all details are verified verbally and followed up with a formal confirmation email; we kindly ask that any cancellations or requests to reschedule be handled via a direct phone call. Because our attorneys’ schedules are built specifically around these appointments, we allow for only one rescheduled consultation following a missed appointment. If a second "no-show" occurs, we will be unable to offer further appointments and will respectfully ask that you seek legal counsel from another firm. In the event of a missed call, our team will leave a voicemail and a follow-up email to reiterate this policy and provide an opportunity to reconnect.

reviews.text:
Hello, I was in a car accident where I was rear ended at a red light last March and not sure what to do. I did have an injury to my lower and upper spine and sought doctors care. I never had an attorney before for a car accident, but I knew I was gonna need to have someone in my corner For guidance to ensure that I was taken care of, both physically, mentally, and my car repaired.
I was referred to Chanfrau and Chanfrau,  From the moment I walked through the door, the staff were very outgoing and polite.
I met with attorney Greg Olsen, and the senior assistant Roxie Courson. They took the time to listen to my story and guided me through what the steps were and answered all my questions. Once we agreed that they were gonna take my case, they took control of taking care of my insurance company the other person‘s insurance company the doctors all the bills and everything else was handled through them.
Throughout the whole journey, Roxie went above and beyond to stay in contact with me and keep me updated and answered other questions that arose.
When all was said and done in the end, Roxie went as far to fight with the doctors offices to get the bills reduced, and actually managed to get me a settlement of more than what I even came close to expect.
I will say that Chanfrau and Chanfrau has a great team, very professional and always available to assist you no matter how big or small the question or issue was that had me concerned. Roxie was a real superstar and on top of everything even though she was going through some personal family issues of her own she managed to perform outstandingly for my settlement. I am very thankful for Chanfrau and Chanfrau being there for me and extra grateful for their superstar, Roxie!
I highly recommend them to anyone in need of legal services, they fight for you not just for a settlement Unlike the horror stories, I’ve heard about other attorneys and law firms. No matter how big or small your case is this firm will fight for you!

I’m incredibly frustrated that my bankruptcy case still has not been submitted. I completed and returned all necessary paperwork on Monday May 13th. Today marks 34 business days since the signed paperwork was returned.
I received an email from Mr. Zippers paralegal on May 27th stating my case would be filed that week, yet here we are on July 2nd — more than a full month and 25 business days later — with no action taken.

This delay is completely UNACCEPTABLE. The lack of communication is UNACCEPTABLE.
I have called multiple times (2-3 times a week) and I receive the same vague response each time... "It is on the Attorneys desk for review", "I will have him call you back", "I'm sorry for the delay, I will give Robert your message again"

If you’re looking for a bankruptcy attorney go somewhere else. He failed to file our bankruptcy paperwork which caused our vehicle to be towed. When I called to ask what happened and why it was towed, they said that we needed to do some credit course. That wasn’t explained to us as something that needed to be done to file. It was explained that it was a survey.
We weren’t given proper communication as to when it needed to be completed or what was happening with our case. He was able to file some emergency paperwork and got our car back.
After a few months of explaining that we were having issues with my wife losing her job and I had another case that I had to get taken care of to save my job, this office never communicated as to what we can do to work with us to continue our case. Our best interest wasn’t taken into consideration and no legal advice was given.
I was asked to pay another $150 to add something to our case, but that again, wasn’t explained well..I thought I was paying so that they could tell the judge our problems.
In October, I woke up to our car being towed again after finding out they closed our case a few days prior because of non payment. I was in contact with this office because I didn’t hear anything from them since our meeting with the trustee back in July. I was asking what we needed to do and why I haven’t heard from them. I was told our case was closed, then a few days later our car was towed again. I then again called and asked why I wasn’t given any instructions on to what needed to be done to file another case. All they did was just sent an email telling me that a refile was what needed to be done. I asked them to contact me with the next steps to go forward with the refile and nothing…our car got towed. Mr Zipperer finally responded to tell us that we never paid him to refile and he couldn’t get our car back a second time.
Sorry sir, but there would have never been a second time if you communicated the first time. And if they communicated how much we needed to pay to refile, we could have done so…If you told us that we needed to do that “survey” and they would have filed the first time, we would have done so…instead I got no phone call, no email, no snail mail….nothing. NO COMMUNICATION! I think they assume everyone has already went through bankruptcy and know what has to be done. Sorry but I’m not a mind reader and don’t know what the cost to refile is or what needs to be done to file a case. I’m not a lawyer…Over all this staff and this attorney are terrible and useless. Their office is unorganized and communication is not in their vocabulary. I filed a complaint with the bar only to find out this isn’t the first time due to a lack of communication. I made a terrible mistake with this guy and now we’re stuck without a vehicle. Enjoy taking selfies and posting them to your Facebook Mr. Zipperer, because when you lose your practice, you’ll be taking more of them with all your free time.

I paid you to represent us and you did not live up to your ethical obligation to do what is in our best interest. Now we have no car and I have to pay someone else to start the process again. Worst lawyer ever…
Update:
Obviously, Mr. Zipperer has a problem reading and comprehending text. I explained in my one star review why we couldn’t pay. The main cause for the one star complaint is the lack of communication. He never exclaimed the “survey” needed to be completed. If that was relayed we would have done so, and our car would have never been towed the first time…his negligence cost us our vehicle. If it never happened the first time, we could have saved it this time.

reviews.text:
I was genuinely hopeful when my godson was appointed a guardian during his custody case. I believed it would finally give him the opportunity to share his perspective with the court. At the time, he was around 16 years old. Unfortunately, over the course of nearly two years, Mr. Ellzey has spent more time and would  rather listen to his father's side instead of actually being there for my Godson

He was suppose to be his voice in court and he was let down numerous times.

Despite my godson clearly expressing to Mr. Ellzey that he felt unsafe with his father. Mr. Ellzey brushed it off repeatedly. These matters ended up being dismissed in court.

At no point did Mr. Ellzey appear to take my godson’s concerns seriously, nor did he act in his best interests or serve as his advocate. —this entire experience was deeply disappointing and I wish no other child to have to go through this.

I was actually really excited when my son was appointed a guardian for his custody case. I thought he would finally have the chance to give his input to the court. My son was about 16 at this time. Mr. Ellzey spent about 5 hours total with my son in almost 2 years. He spent over 25 hours with his father, though.... my son told Mr. Ellzey he was unsafe with his father. Mr. Ellzey laughed about it in court and said my son was just strong willed. My son wrote Mr. Ellzey a letter outlining all of the crimes his father had committed... Mr. Ellzey laughed about it in court and said he should become a lawyer. Never once did Mr. Ellzey take anything my son was saying seriously and he definitely had no concern for my son's interests or being my son's voice. Add to that insanely high hourly fees and allowing his father to stack up charges that I had to pay half of just to keep talking and meeting for no reason and this was an extremely disappointing experience.

I cannot say enough great things about everyone at Buckmaster and Ellzey. I struggled with my divorce for 2 years and was let down by other attorneys previously.

Everyone here listen to my situation and worked with me to get everything finally resolved. They went above and beyond to make sure that I had the assistance that I needed to finalize everything.

I highly recommend them and wish I had found them sooner.

It was a scary day indeed when a relative of ours relayed to us the trouble he had gotten himself into.  He had always been a good caring soul who, it appeared, had been taken advantage of.

A detective was now wanting to interview our relative and we knew we needed some professional help and FAST.  Through a friend we found Attorney Buckmaster.

The moment we met him, a sense of peace came upon us.  He was such a good listener and laid out the good, the bad and the ugly of how things could go.

It was now a waiting game between Attorney Buckmaster and the Sheriff’s office.  Within 8 days, the situation was resolved and we all breathed a sigh of relief.

We knew going in this would not be easy for Attorney Buckmaster, but we trusted him and knew he would do his very best.  His staff which included Laney set our minds at ease with any questions we had, and she sent out her Friday summary as she said she always does for clients.

We will forever be grateful to Attorney Buckmaster and his staff.
Jeremy’s the man. He gets things done. Extremely satisfied with the outcome of my case and his law firm.

I would pick this firm over and over again if I ever needed it (although, I would not recommend going through another divorce - 0/10 stars). Attorney Cantrell and Paralegal Morgan were wonderful and helpful in so many ways, I wish I had them from the start. Att. Cantrell laid out all my options and never once did I feel blindsided by the situation I was in or feel like he didn't have my back. Do it, get an attorney for your divorce and pick this firm and save yourself the headache. They know what they are doing.

I couldn’t have asked for a more compassionate, empathetic, experienced, professional, subject matter expert than the folks employed at this firm! Not only did they take on my case late in the game but they got up to speed and had future plans before our consultation and had answers to all of my questions during this same meeting. I will be recommending this firm to anyone and everyone that needs these types of services. Excellent experience! I’m incredibly thankful not only for this level of service but for how they helped me set up my future life!
After almost 5 years of Darren not seeing his daughter. He finally signed over his rights. So I guess we will b seeing u in court again.

reviews.text: On October 10, 2024, I retained Daytona Defense Attorney to cover a case that had not yet been filed. I paid the $4000 retainer fee upfront. Itturns out the charges never did get filed and so I let them know that they didn’t need to do anything, and if I can get refund, that would be Fantastic. They then agreed over the phone to give me back $2500 of my $4000 even though they didn’t do one thing. This was December 24, 2024. I’ve yet to see a dime in now. The guy in charge his name is Jeff over there is telling methat he never said $2500 and he’s only gonna give me back $2000 even though they didn’t file one paper or do anything for my case the case didn’t exist it was never filed
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ohhhhhhh, Richard! Bless your little heart! You left so many important details out of your review. Like the fact that when you hired us, you signed a contract agreeing that the fee you agreed to pay was NON-REFUNDABLE. Or the fact that by hiring us before you were actually arrested - A VERY smart move, by the way! - we were able to ensure that your case was resolved without charges being filed or you being arrested. (A SPECTACULAR result by one of our top young attorneys, Brian Penney.)

Lots of people would have the good sense and the gratitude to appreciate that they had dodged a legal bullet & happily moved on with their lives. But you - even after agreeing that the fee you paid was non-refundable - thought a refund was in order.

In the interest of good will, we agreed to provide a 50% refund- $2000! - and even sent you a check for that amount. (In fact, even with all your bluster, I'm willing to bet you put your pride in the back seat & casehd that check. That's how guys like you roll.)

When you texted back & insisted that the agreement was for a refund of $2500, I informed you that all of the records and texts that we had reflected an agreement to refund $2000. HOWEVER, because we are a high-performing law firm that understands the importance of EVIDENCE & how it can be used to prove a point, I agreed to refund $2500 if you could provide ANY evidence that we ever agreed to that number. You could have produced a text, a voicemail, an email, another witness......anything at all. But you were unable to produce any evidence to support your position. And when you realized your lie was being exposed, you insulted my character & integrity, despite the fact that I had agreed to give you a refund of half your fee & the opportunity to prove that we had agreed to refund more.

Later, when you accused me of not standing behind my word, I provided you a copy of the fee agreement you negotiated and signed. I pointed out to you that in that agreement, you agreed that the fee was non-refundable, and that by asking for a refund AFTER the job was done, YOU were actually the one refusing to stand behind his word.

Lots of people in your position would have the gratitutde to be pleased that they were the beneficiary of a job well done. Many of our clients would happily trade places with you, if it meant they could have avoided jail. But it doesn't seem like you have that level of mental or emotional maturity or grace to recognize when good things happen to you. My guess is that those shortcomings may explain a lot of the struggles you've been facing in life. I hope you're able to use the $2000 we refunded to you to get those problems professionally addressed. It would probably do you & the people around you a lot of good.

Finally, I noticed that as of today (April 21, 2025), you have left 18 reviews on Google. Of those 18, five have been 1-star reviews. Of those five 1-star reviews, four of them have been left in the LAST TWO WEEKS. I'm no expert, but if every one that YOU have encountered in the last two weeks deserves a 1-star review, maybe YOU are a bigger part of your problems than you realize.

Good luck. I hope you get better. And I'm sure everyone who has to deal with you hopes you get better, too. Goodness knows, they probably deserve the break.

reviews.text:
They sent me mail for another person in an entirely different city with a completely different DOB saying I was arrested for battery on the 10th and gave a spiel about how “this could ruin my life” which seems innately predatory considering the wording and phrasing they used in said mail. Scammers often use urgency to make a person feel pressured into becoming a scam victim without them being any wiser by circumventing any possibility for rational thinking by saying you have a finite amount of time before major, life altering consequences occur. I ended up looking up the person who the mail was for and he was indeed arrested for battery but I’m not him nor do I appreciate spam mail from a company who isn’t competent enough to find the correct address of the person they’re soliciting, especially considering it’s public record and their entire profession and basis for their business.

If these guys are lawyers I’d steer clear because if they lack the basic, fundamental understanding of reading through arrest records so much so that they send mail to the wrong person when two people share vaguely similar yet extremely common names I’d imagine they’d be great at accidentally leaking your personal information to the wrong people with 0 due diligence regarding retrieving proper credentials and would also show up to court with a portfolio for an entirely different person. The fact I’ve never used their “services” yet am getting spam in my mail box for another person entirely shows me why I and anyone who’s considering them should refrain from using their services AT ALL.

In response to the owners reply:
If this company representing people thinks their company is a meme then that’s fine and I guess you’re entitled to feel however you want. I personally wouldn’t want someone who thinks the legal system is a joke or someone who’s claiming they have the technological knowhow to have an automated system send mail on their behalf but aren’t able to tell said system to distinguish addresses to represent me in court but again I’m not here to control what people do nor do I believe in doing that, I’m just here spreading awareness on an issue I’ve had prior to even contacting this business. If you’d like help making decent systems I have an abundance of experience working with different systems both with hardware and software, so feel free to reach out and I’ll send over my resume.
reviews.ownerResponse:
To paraphrase a poplular meme: This review could have been a phone call.

We apologize for you receiving a letter incorrectly addressed to you. If you had simply called in, we could have put your address on our "do not mail" list. Although you may not realize, these letters are sent out by computer software & mistakes happen from time to time. As you mentioned in your review, you share the same name as the individual to whom the letter was addressed. So I'm sure you can understand how such a mistake could take place.

Luckily, from the sounds of things, you were not negatively impacted by the mistake in this case & that is a good thing. Please keep us in mind if you ever have a need for our services.

reviews.text:
I spoke with about half a dozen lawyers, some in person, including one I didn’t feel particularly confident about. Attorney Higgins called me within an hour of submitting my details on their website Daytona Defense. He laid out a strategic approach, emphasizing experience and excellence at the table.

My friend had been dealing with a situation that seemed like common sense for nearly six months. However, both the public defender and the prosecutor appeared inexperienced. Attorney Higgins did an incredible job assembling a team that secured my friend's release and achieved the best possible outcome within a week.

I cannot stress enough that common sense doesn’t always carry weight in legal matters.

I've hired Jeff five times due to his effectiveness. It's as if all the people who run the courts like this guy and when you hire him to represent you a possible mandatory prison sentence turns into probation. His rates are reasonable and he gets exceptional results. I don't just give out trust. Actions and the little things talk and all I've ever seen from Jeff is good character. I trust his advice because I truly believe it's not all about the money with him.  This attorney did not rush to plea out, nor did I get charged with any extra expenses. Through all negotiations I never had to appear in court. I was offered a decent deal from the start and a second offer that was even better. I told Mr Higgins what was important to me and he secured me a verdict that fit my needs . Don't bother looking any place else or spending a ton of money. Jeff gets results and the problems go away.

Daytona Defense, Jeffery Higgins, and Tammy Jaques have been true heroes. They were able to get my foster son’s outrageous charge of a second degree felony reduced to misdemeanors. The public defender he originally had was just going to let him get slammed in court. They were fast acting, intelligent, and got the job done as it should be. I highly recommend this firm to anyone who needs a competent fast acting defense team.

The law enforcement offer I was concerned about had his car parked in the rear of the building so it appeared as though the attorney was working for him and still took my money. Six months went by and I never heard a word or received anything from the firm. The attorney seemed to at least listen. So I returned 6 months later when I was feeling better and said “thanks for at least listening” even though I received zero services for my money. The exit of the office was most strange when this grown male attorney literally asked me if he could give me a hugging and wrapped his arms around me. reviews.ownerResponse:
J, we’re sorry you’re dissatisfied with our service, but there are lots of reasons why a Trust may not be the best option to transfer assets in the most cost effective and efficient manner; which is what we strive for for every client regardless of asset level.

Trusts are expensive to set up and usually the fee involved for administration is comparable to that of probate. We look at the totality of a client’s situation before making a recommendation, even if they’re sent to us with a preconceived notion of what they “need.”

Best of luck with your endeavors and law school. With permission from your mother, I encourage you to call in and discuss this with one of our Estate Planning attorneys directly so you can better understand the particular recommendations.

reviews.text: I hired Smith and Eulo for a DUI that I did not do. They assigned Attorney Zaleski to my case and he did absolutely nothing for me. Right from the beginning he told me not to request an administrative review for my license because he said that is just a formality. I told him how I wasn’t driving and that my boyfriend was driving. I asked him to get the video from the parking lot that he pulled into because it would show him driving. He said that they record over them every 3 days. He didn’t even try to get any videos. He could have even requested the video from the traffic light at the entrance of the parking lot that would show that i wasn’t driving. At the beginning he even was talking about a reckless driving and probation. He never intended to try and prove my innocence. I used money that was for to bury my mom’s ashes on hiring them and it was a waste of money. He talked me into taking a plea deal to get rid of this case,  he scared me into it because he just wanted the case done. Now I am on probation for a crime that I didn’t commit because he didn’t want to do anything to prove that I wasn’t driving.

I cannot say enough good things about this attorney. He is truly an amazing family law attorney and has been incredibly helpful throughout the entire process. He always keeps me in the loop, explains everything clearly, and makes sure I understand what’s going on at every step.

I really appreciate how honest and straightforward he is, he doesn’t sugarcoat anything, which I value so much in a situation like this. He tells you the truth, sets realistic expectations, and genuinely has your best interest in mind.

So glad I came across Osuna Law while searching for a divorce attorney!
I was beyond impressed when Mr. Osuna discussed all the steps involved in the divorce process during our initial phone conversation. He’s extremely knowledgeable and his expertise and understanding of the law were evident as he answered all my questions. His professionalism, kindness and empathy quickly put my mind at ease.
He is dedicated, hardworking and his meticulous attention to detail showed in the impeccable legal documents I received.
Mr. Osuna is an exceptional attorney and I can’t thank him enough for helping me achieve such a favorable outcome in my divorce settlement! He greatly exceeded my expectations and l’ll always be grateful for his excellent representation!
I highly recommend Mr. Osuna to anyone seeking a compassionate and highly skilled attorney!

When you’re in the worst of times and need a lawyer you can trust in Giovanni.

I was in the absolute darkest of my days trying to navigate a divorce as well as an injunction for protection from domestic violence and it felt like I had no one to turn to for help.

When I spoke with Giovanni, his transparency, kindness and professionalism made it clear he was the right choice.

Giovanni did in all of 3 days what I had been fighting to do for a year. My divorce is now done. My injunction granted. And I get to start a new life where I am free and safe and there’s no amount of stars in a review to really convey my appreciation nor the excellence of his work and the passion he has for his clients.

If you’re ever in need of an attorney, I highly recommend Giovanni.

Well, one of the reasons I reached out to this attorney was because of the great reviews! From past experience, I decided to contact three different attorneys before I made my decision. This was my last attorney‘s office to contact. It took us a few days to get in touch with each other and I’m dealing with a time sensitive matter. I got off work a little late and had to pick up my four-year-old from daycare. I missed our scheduled call because of this. I’m a hairstylist and sometimes appointments run over. I wanted to get back in touch with him right away, so he knew I was a serious client and wasn’t trying to blow him off.

Something to note: my son is a handful and has melt downs quite frequently. He’s literally having one as we speak. He has emotional/behavioral complications. Anyways I continued to explain to this Attorney my situation. My son was having one of his many meltdowns in the background. I apologized profusely throughout the call but assumed he would understand that a busy working Mom that needed to find an attorney ASAP has to work with what she’s got. It’s messy and chaotic, but that’s life!

After pouring my heart out to him and explaining the entirety of my situation… Which I had to be super vulnerable and open and honest… He literally responds to me like this. I said, “So what are your thoughts on everything I just explained to you?” His response, “My first question would be do you think it’s appropriate to call me with your four-year-old upset in the background?” Honestly, I was shocked! Apparently he has no experience dealing with real life people and real life situations. If I had the luxury of waiting until my fiancé came home to watch him so I could have peace and quiet then we would never speak because he gets off work way past this attorney‘s office hours.

One of the first attorneys I contacted actually had to talk to me through a mini crisis because my dog ran away while we were doing our consultation. They met me with grace, compassion, kindness, and understanding. Like you would hope an attorney would respond. My son was also being quite the handful that day as well. They never even mentioned it.

So needless to say, I told him that we would not be a good match because of obvious reasons. I’d much rather pay $6000 to an attorney that will fight for me and meet me where I’m at. Sometimes that means being on a phone call with a pain in the butt four-year-old in the background. Again, just dumbfounded! Do yourself a favor and invest in good representation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback! As a parent, I’m sure you can understand that driving with an upset child makes it very hard to focus on the road, care for the needs of your child, all the while discussing such important legal matters. Not to mention the safety risk you can put yourself and your child in by juggling all three at the same time. My question to you, that you misstated likely due to not being able to hear me as I had an incredibly hard time hearing you as you can imagine, was simply geared towards trying to reschedule the call at a later time that was more convenient for you so that you could give your legal matter the focus and attention that it deserves. If you would like to reschedule please give us a call I’d be happy to find a time that works best for your busy schedule. We look forward to hearing from you!

reviews.text:
Not too long ago, we went through a very difficult and unfortunate situation involving a breakup with a former partner, and it was the first time we ever needed to work with a lawyer. Hertel Legal stood out in a way we hadn’t experienced before.

What meant the most to us was that we never once felt like a dollar sign. It was clear they had taken the time to read through everything we provided and came prepared to every meeting. They showed genuine compassion, care, and a real commitment to defending us. Throughout the entire process, we truly felt like they had our back and it was extra reassuring that they could go to court as well if needed.

Ron was professional, grounded, and sincere, and always made us feel that our best interests were his top priority. Vanessa was incredibly organized, responsive, and compassionate — both always on top of every detail and quick to follow up. The fact that the firm is bilingual was also incredibly important for us, as it helped my husband feel fully understood and comfortable during an already stressful time.

They are a family-run firm with strong values, excellent communication, and a very human approach to what they do. They stayed in touch, followed up consistently, and treated us with respect and care every step of the way.

We highly recommend Hertel Legal and would absolutely, without hesitation, use their services again if ever needed.
Hertel legal is absolutely the best option for legal services I could ever recommend.

Michael Rodriguez was a phenomenal attorney for my family law matter. He was always clear with me about the procedure and talked me through possible outcomes at each stage. While I hope I never have to go back to litigate custody again, if I do, I know who I'm using!

Attorney Michael Rodriguez represented me during my divorce proceedings. Ending a marriage after 40 years was very challenging and often intimidating for me, emotionally as well as financially, but Michael was by my side every step of the way.

He patiently answered all of my questions regarding the legal process as they arose, and would always respond in a timely manner by telephone or e-mail. When he was unable to respond himself, his very efficient and personable legal assistant Morgan would relay his responses, and she would periodically provide updates related to my case.

Michael’s professionalism, compassion and sensitivity to my concerns gave  me confidence that my interests were always his top priority, and he ultimately achieved a very favorable settlement on my behalf. I can recommend him without hesitation or reservation to anyone who has need of a divorce attorney who cares. Thank you so much Michael and Morgan!

reviews.text: I hired Attorney Johnson in 2014 before I knew this website existed. I want to leave a review for that time when I used his services. I fell down the stairs at work and was bedridden for several weeks. After all the doctor's appointments and private investigations, I started losing my mind about it all. It was overwhelming and I ended up losing my house because I was out of work. The insurance companies were claiming I wasn't injured and it got messy. Attorney Johnson got the private investigating to cease, as well as got me the correct medical treatment. What the insurance companies tried to write off as nothing, Attorney Johnson was able to get me compensation for. I was so very pleased with my case and working with Attorney Johnson, I have rehired him for current car accident case which I will review when it's closed.

Lance Gilbert had zero empathy for my denial for Parkinson's, my condition or any positive outlook.  Did not consider anything I had to say and kept quoting SS as if he worked for them.  I have a better understanding of listing 11.06 than him, which he had to look up while we spoke.  Never offered to help and told me I can appeal for reconsideration  myself and wait for a hearing in 2020.  All you did was kick somebody when they're down.  I would not even give you one star but I have to to make this post.
I see your response after first initially deleting my review.  I have been trained in empathy and report in my customer service experience which would be of benefit to you with future free telephone consultations.  I have also spoken with another attorney who offered an approach to my appeal reconsideration different from the "you can do the same thing as we can".  People shop around for attorneys the same as you would for anything, especially something as important as this.
reviews.ownerResponse: I was sorry to see your review following your free telephone consultation.  I sincerely apologize if I appeared to lack empathy regarding your case; that was not my intention.  Given that I do not represent you and had never spoken with you before, I was simply trying to gather as much pertinent information about your case as I could in a short period of time by being direct and to the point.  With this information I could offer insight based on my experience in this district on what you could possibly expect as you move forward and what steps could be taken to improve your chances for a successful claim.  My knowledge of what the judges of this district typically look for in a claim is a large factor in approx. 90% of my claims being approved this year compared to the district’s approval average of 40%.  Following our consult, I sent you a questionnaire that may assist in your case.  I wish you the best of luck in your claim.

reviews.text: I can honestly say I have encountered many attorneys who have represented me in the past, but I have had by far the best experience with Matt Foxman as well as his paralegal, Danielle Martin. He took the time out to actually know me as a person; he saw I had good intentions and recognized that. When someone is representing you, they have to believe in you to be willing to actively fight for something they wish to have accomplished. He also reassured me that the path I walk in life is honorable and made me aware I should be proud of my accomplishments and achievements. In addition, I found it admirable that he did not automatically recommend to go against, meaning to file another motion or attack. Instead, he guided and influenced me to diffuse and find a common ground to settle for peace. I believe that due to those actions, everything will subside and work out from my good act of faith. Mr. Doran was very thorough, detailed,  and did excellent work  cleaning up the mess of my prior attorney. All the while, fighting for my best interest. reviews.text:
If you are reading this review while searching for an attorney, stop, pick up the phone and call the Doran firm now.

I had no idea what to expect when suddenly and unexpectedly I found myself needing a divorce lawyer. A good friend recommended Ted Doran and I was able to set an appointment almost immediately. I was in shock, afraid, and very confused after finding out my 40 year marriage was ending. My husband and I owned a lucrative business together and were also involved in separate businesses with both of our grown children. There was a lot to untangle and I was terrified of ending up with nothing.

The outcome was better than I could have imagined and it was 100% due to the team at Ted Doran’s office. Ted was always patient, calm and available for any question. Night or day. He even gave me his cell phone number in case of any emergency. I was amazed at the number of experts he was able to call on when it became clear we would be going to trial and needed to prove income and dispute large discrepancies in valuations done by the other side.
Teds paralegal Dani is amazing. Her incredible organization skills, attention to detail, patience and understanding combine to make her perfect for her career choice.
Your phone call will be answered by Donna who always has a smile and welcomes you warmly and by name from your first visit through to the end of your case.

Attorney Carol Yoon with her ability to explain complicated financial statements, investment and tax laws (just one of her many talents) in a way that I could easily understand made it possible for me to complete necessary documents and understand the circumstances enough to make informed decisions regarding my future after divorce.

I have nothing but good things to say about every person I dealt with at this firm.  I would recommend them with the highest confidence.

Angela Erickson

I highly recommend Ted Doran. He was very kind and professional throughout my entire case. He made sure everything was handled smoothly and in a timely manner, which I truly appreciated. Beth

As a result of thorough analysis and recommendations from my friends, I approached the firm Doran Sims Wolfe & Yoon to prepare the documentation related to establishing a trust. I found myself in a challenging situation, but my lawyer, Mr. Sims, supported me through this ordeal with remarkable diligence and understanding.

We had several in-person meetings, but most of our correspondence was conducted via email, and I do not recall a single question of mine going unanswered or not receiving sound advice. I often wrote at 1 or 2 AM, and I always received a comprehensive response from him the following day.

Additionally, I can say that the quality of the documents is simply exceptional; the people in the office are very friendly and strive to assist in any situation. Regarding payment, believe me, it is absolutely justified and much less than at places where I inquired.

To such highly professional specialists and wonderful people like Mr. Sims, I wish great health and prosperity, so they can continue to bring joy to others.

reviews.text:
My ex-wife moved to Daytona. I needed a good lawyer so I looked up Carol Yoon. She gave me a great 1st consultation and I paid my $5000 retainer fee for her representation. I can only speak about my personal experience in family court against my ex wife. Carol Yoon and her coworker Danielle immediately gathered all the facts of my case.

They positioned my case into a winning position without much work. My ex-wife was trying to get the courts to pay double the amount for 1 child. Instead Carol and Dani turned the tables on my ex-wife which made her lawyer drop out. After that humiliation, my ex-wife paid another lawyer to represent her. This time the opposition didn’t quit, but understood they had nothing to bargain from, so my ex was forced to take a fair amount of child support, agreed upon by both of us. Carol and Danielle work so good as a team, they have no real weaknesses that I witnessed.

So I highly recommend Carol and Danielle for a child support battle. The money was well spent. Choosing Carol Yoon and Dani Garay was the wisest choice I’ve made thus far. Both are relentless litigators, who work tirelessly to get good results. No short cuts were taken.

I truly don’t believe there is another attorney out there like Ron Zimmet. His kindness is unmatched. He is incredibly compassionate and genuinely cares—not just about your case, but about you as a person.

From the very beginning, Mr. Zimmet took the time to really listen to me. I was so afraid that parts of my past—things I’ve worked hard to overcome—would negatively impact my case. But instead of judging me, he focused on how far I’ve come and even celebrated my progress. That meant the world to me.

He consistently checked in, kept me updated, and made sure I never felt alone during the entire process. Sometimes, he’d listen to me talk about things that had nothing to do with my case—just because he genuinely cared. That kind of empathy is rare.

Mr. Zimmet worked tirelessly to stand up to the insurance company on my behalf. I am beyond grateful for everything he has done for me.

Yes, he is an amazing attorney. But more importantly, he is an amazing human being.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind words. You are a wonderful person. I truly enjoyed representing you and getting to know you.  I’m very grateful that you placed your trust in us and I’m grateful that we had the opportunity to help.

reviews.text:
I'll never know if Mr. Trovato is a good attorney because of the experience I had with the person taking the calls.

I was on notice from the first word out of her mouth, which was simply "Hello?" It didn't sound like I'd reached a law office, so I said, "I'm sorry, who am I talking to?" and she just said her first name. I said I was trying to reach a business and wasn't sure I had. Only then did she seem to switch into work mode.

She wanted to connect me to the attorney for my consultation then, but I an appointment, so she told me to call the next afternoon at any point because he had nothing scheduled.

Within 20 minutes, she called back to let me know he was available right then, as if the conversation we'd just had that had never happened. I reiterated that I'd have to call the next day and double-checked the hours with her.

When I called the following day, it was the same person. She did answer with the name of the business this time! She said she was sorry, but he wasn't in the office that afternoon but would be Monday or Tuesday.

I said I was told several times by her (I had to ask for her name) that he'd be there. The gist of her response was , sorry not sorry, stuff happens.

It was only after the first call that I read the other reviews, and noticed that the negative ones were mostly about the staff, not the lawyer and I completely understand why.

I highly recommend Mr. Trovato to anyone needing a wise, reliable family law attorney—especially if your case involves unusual or complex circumstances. From the beginning, he was incredibly straightforward and clear, which gave me confidence in what to expect throughout the process. His honesty, professionalism, and compassion made a difficult situation feel more manageable. Beyond being a skilled attorney, he’s also a genuinely good person who truly cares about his clients. I’m grateful for everything he’s done for me and my family.

He agreed to schedule the date for a Thursday and when I tell him that the person who will call will be my husband, he tells me that the lawyer can't talk to him because he has to talk to the person on the case. Terrific family law attorney who knows the law and the local courts very well.  I’ve been through a number of mediations with my former wife over the years with her (in my view) always getting more than what was fair, and right for our child.  Recently, she relocated out of State and wanted to take our child with her, so back at it we went.  I stumbled across George by happenstance, never used him previously and didn’t know anything about him other than Google reviews.  In my very first conversation with George, I could tell he knew family law very well.  He told me exactly how this relocation effort would be viewed by the court and how it should play out.  Well, 5 months later when we walked out of the courthouse, things went quite literally exactly as he said they would 5 months earlier.  This result, however, did not happen on its own.  My former wife hired a decorated attorney that aggressively pursued every tactic possible and invaded every corner of my life, looking for any angle to turn the situation his client’s way.  What I learned about George is that he is a street fighter.  He calmly and effortlessly maneuvered us through the process.  I always felt like we were a couple steps ahead, and we were.  He is honest to a fault but always willing to take the time to explain things which helped to keep me at ease.  He told me what things I should worry about, and what not to worry about.  His toughness is what I lacked in the past.  He’s exactly who I needed for this moment.  Client for life.

Hello, I was in a car accident where I was rear ended at a red light last March and not sure what to do. I did have an injury to my lower and upper spine and sought doctors care. I never had an attorney before for a car accident, but I knew I was gonna need to have someone in my corner For guidance to ensure that I was taken care of, both physically, mentally, and my car repaired.
I was referred to Chanfrau and Chanfrau,  From the moment I walked through the door, the staff were very outgoing and polite.
I met with attorney Greg Olsen, and the senior assistant Roxie Courson. They took the time to listen to my story and guided me through what the steps were and answered all my questions. Once we agreed that they were gonna take my case, they took control of taking care of my insurance company the other person‘s insurance company the doctors all the bills and everything else was handled through them.
Throughout the whole journey, Roxie went above and beyond to stay in contact with me and keep me updated and answered other questions that arose.
When all was said and done in the end, Roxie went as far to fight with the doctors offices to get the bills reduced, and actually managed to get me a settlement of more than what I even came close to expect.
I will say that Chanfrau and Chanfrau has a great team, very professional and always available to assist you no matter how big or small the question or issue was that had me concerned. Roxie was a real superstar and on top of everything even though she was going through some personal family issues of her own she managed to perform outstandingly for my settlement. I am very thankful for Chanfrau and Chanfrau being there for me and extra grateful for their superstar, Roxie!
I highly recommend them to anyone in need of legal services, they fight for you not just for a settlement Unlike the horror stories, I’ve heard about other attorneys and law firms. No matter how big or small your case is this firm will fight for you!

reviews.text:
I have retained Jane Park as my family law attorney since 2021, to 2026 and I offer my highest recommendation for her legal services. This case was prolonged, emotionally complex, and highly contentious, involving an opposing party who repeatedly attempted to use our child as leverage and weaponize her against me. Throughout the entirety of this process, Mrs. Park demonstrated exceptional professionalism, reliability, and integrity to help me keep this monster of a baby daddy (BIG Narcissist) away from my young daughter.

Mrs. Park was consistently responsive and took the time to clearly explain legal procedures and options, particularly during moments when I was understandably overwhelmed or uncertain. Her ability to communicate complex legal matters with clarity and patience was invaluable. She conducted herself with kindness and respect toward both me and my family, never losing sight of the human element involved in family law matters.

Through Mrs. Park’s diligence, strategic advocacy, and unwavering commitment, we ultimately prevailed. Most importantly, my daughter’s voice was heard, and her legal identity was restored through a successful last name change. This outcome has had a profound and lasting impact on our lives.

Mrs. Park’s representation went far beyond legal advocacy; she was a steady and principled presence during one of the most difficult periods of my life. She did not simply win a case—she protected a child’s best interests and helped secure her future.
THANK YOU SO MUCH JANE!
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow, Melanee, I'm so moved by your beautiful review! I feel protective of you and your family as I would my own, and it warms my heart to think I've contributed to your, and your child's, happiness. We've seen some positive outcomes along the way and also endured some heart-wrenching events, but here's hoping you and your child are on the way to the wonderful future you richly deserve! As always, let me know if you need any further help.

reviews.text: I want to express my deepest gratitude to my expert lawyer Jane Parker, for her outstanding support and dedication throughout my case. Thanks to her knowledge, patience, and constant communication, She is always available attending calls and emails herself unlike other lawyers who takes your money then you chase them for communications through their assistants I truly appreciate her hard work, cooperation, and commitment. And I also want to Thank Mr Matt her husband for his services. I highly recommend Jane to anyone who wants an expert lawyer with affordable price. Best In Florida!
reviews.ownerResponse: Pola, I'm so happy to have been able to help! I just know when I've met good people who deserve a fair shake. Thank you so much for leaving such a wonderful review, and Matt thanks you as well! It was our honor to represent you.

reviews.text:
I honestly cannot recommend Jane Park enough. Since the very first meeting, I KNEW I had to have Jane in my corner through what I knew would be an extremely tumultuous divorce.

First and foremost, Jane understood my urgency (and anxiety) and because of this, she communicated with me frequently. She's very diligent and thorough. She prepared me for ALL the what ifs and- during the couple of unfortunate times when things didn't go my way- she continued to strategize and fight for me until we persevered.

Jane is honestly one of the most steadfast human beings I've ever met and above all, also one of the most compassionate. You can absolutely tell that she cares deeply for her clients and fights tooth and nail to get the outcomes we need and deserve.

I knew my divorce would be chaotic and complicated but little did I know just how INSANE things would become. It was a rollercoaster ride and Jane- was my safety harness. I am forever grateful that she continuously fought (and had the patience) for me and my child like we were her own family.

She's absolutely dedicated to her profession and worked tirelessly for me until the very last day. I never doubted hiring Jane as my divorce attorney for one second.

Thank you Jane!
reviews.ownerResponse: Lindsay, your kind words brought me to tears. I absolutely feel the responsibility toward my clients that I would feel towards a family member. When things don't go right, I often agonize, but I always then wrack my brain and do the research to try to find another way. I consider it an honor that my clients entrust me with their, and their children's, futures, and I'll always fight to get them what they deserve, and, hopefully, more than that. You and the other self-sacrificing parents of the world are the backbone of our collective future, and I'm grateful for all of you. Wishing you nothing but the best, and I'm here if you need me again, of course!

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough great things about Jane Park. She has supported me through multiple complex and emotional legal situations over the past two years. She represented me in an injunction hearing where I was granted everything I requested, helping ensure my safety and peace of mind during a very difficult time.

When a last-minute shelter hearing came up involving DCF, a matter outside her usual specialty, Jane stepped in without hesitation. She did an incredible job and secured a positive ruling from the judge when I truly had no one else to turn to.

She then guided me through my divorce, which has now been finalized with an outcome I am very happy with. Looking back, I’m even grateful the process took time, because it gave me space to think through what I needed not just in the moment, but for the long-term. Jane made sure those long-term protections were included in the final agreement.

Throughout everything, Jane has been knowledgeable, dedicated, and compassionate. She truly cared about my future and fought for the best results every step of the way. I am finally on the other side of this long journey, and I’m so thankful she was there with me. I highly recommend Jane to anyone who needs a strong and caring advocate. She is a great attorney!

reviews.text: Jane had to deal with my tumultuous divorce from a less than respectable person. It was complicated by a significant amount of money and power being used by the opposition. She had to fight, tooth and nail, for every single victory. She was smart about planning and preparation for the multitude of trials and hearings. Jane was supportive, which was the only thing that carried me through on rough days. She was kind and empathetic. She didn’t give up even though I wanted to throw in the towel. The cards seemed stacked against me, but thanks to her dedication and professional excellence, she helped me and my children survive a nightmare and emerge victorious. The justice system will never know who you are, what really happened or if you deserve what you are asking for. That’s why it is important to have an amazing attorney to help the judge understand and make the right decision. Jane Park is that attorney. Gods speed to those still in the throes of divorce. You are in my prayers.
reviews.ownerResponse: I was so happy to help you and all the good people who become my clients!  What didn't kill us made us stronger!  Thank you so much for your wonderful review, and I wish nothing but the best for you and your family!

reviews.text:
I did not have a good experience.  Very unprofessional.  After $3500 and one year later, I still have no rights to see my child except two days a month, (which aren’t enforced).  My daughter has been abused under her current guardian and now I am even further away than I was before, I walked in that office.  Take that for what it’s worth.   I was also threatened to be sued for slander, if I were to disclose or post anything slanderous about my case or ex-attorney.  I figure since advertising is a way to drum up business;  It only seems right to be able to leave recommendations or opinions as long as they are not made up or fiction based. I am free under the first amendment Of the constitution of the United States of America to give my recommendations or opinions.  She is also given the same platform to rebuke or make a case for herself if she feel the need.  I did not ask for this representation but I guess it’s hard to find good help?  We can’t  all have great reviews all the time because we are not great all the time.  Sincerely.

I guess that’s why the Florida bar is involved they found it was serious  enough to pursue?  Whether you disagree or not, Will be seeing an arbitrator, Let them decide.  Stay away

reviews.text:
This firm has shown a complete lack of professionalism, respect, and client support. I was wrongfully accused of being rude, which is entirely false. Over the course of several years, my case was handled without me ever having the opportunity to speak directly with an attorney only staff.

When I called to advocate for myself and raise concerns, I was informed by a staff member that my case had been withdrawn due to alleged rudeness. At no point was I disrespectful. Clients have the right to ask questions and express concerns when they do not understand what is happening with their case.

What is most troubling is that the attorney representing me for years contacted me for the first time only after withdrawing from my case. During that conversation, when I attempted to express myself and ask for clarification, he made inappropriate comments about my age, implying that it was relevant. My age has absolutely nothing to do with this matter and bringing it up was unprofessional and insulting.

It is unacceptable for an attorney to argue with a client for seeking clarification, especially after years of representation, and then expect payment. While a firm has the right to withdraw from representation, it is wrong to retain a case for years, fail to communicate, and then withdraw based on false claims.

This experience has been deeply disappointing. You never know what clients are dealing with in their personal lives, and dismissing them in this manner especially by making comments about age is unacceptable. This firm’s conduct was unprofessional, disrespectful, and misleading.
Never seen an office that doesn’t communicate with their clients and then decides to drop the client with a disability hearing next month!! Don’t waste your time!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Bowden, we have been trying to contact you for months. You failed to keep in communication with our office.

🤦‍♂️You will feel like according to a few other reviews they are either working against you or with the opposing counsel or you are fighting for yourself

Its not just a "feeling" they have the tendency to use "timing" or psychological pressure to get you to do things seemingly against your interest

As per email chains that I have:

#1 They used the threat of withdrawal to convince me to voluntarily dismiss my case instead of filing a response to "a drafted motion of summary of judgement ". Now I have to scramble and learn how to legal respond withing 20 days.

#2 Used psychological pressure to try and force me to sign a medical authorization (initially with a very broad scope, until I had them reduce it) I notified them multiple times that had the wrong date of birth.

- Also sent a mailed document stating "The judge will MAKE me sign those authorizations, even with the wrong date of birth

-Date of birth was corrected, but months after and once again with poor timing to the point where it could have gotten this in trouble for the delay

- During the hearing on their withdrawals they made claims to the judge there was no "dispute of genuine fact when there are at  least SEVERAL to justify their departure.

Words from the JUDGE Friday October 31 2025: Agreed that their timing to withdraw after 16 months, right now before mediation and court next month was "I agree and don't like this at all".

They had multiple reasons for withdrawal and claimed to proceed, they would be engaging in fraud. Which is false I documented and clearly disputed that I desired them to tell the truth and be seen by a jury.

They did APOLAGIZE to me and the judge after being asked "why now"

Tips: Document everything with them in email and if they are recording phone calls follow up with email or record as well, don't sign incomplete or incorrect forms with BROAD authorizations, and make sure to label each page "1 of 1, 1of 2" etc.

I can help you if you need more info regarding this organization.
