I WISH I COULD GIVE NEGATIVE STARS!!!!The Law Offices of Rotstein & Shiffman represented my husband in his disability case starting in January 2022. He was unable to work due to medical issues as of June 2021 and underwent around six surgeries in that first year. Communicating with the attorney was extremely frustrating; we could never reach them or receive a callback. He couldn't collect unemployment because he was applying for disability, and he couldn't work for the same reason. Additionally, his ex was constantly trying to have him jailed over child support, which only eased when he was no longer working.

He provided medical care for his children and had to cover the costs of various medications and specialist visits every two weeks. After being repeatedly denied for disability and struggling to communicate with the attorney, he returned to work in January 2024. However, the nature of the work available to him after such a long absence only exacerbated his medical condition, leading to more surgeries. He felt he had no choice but to push through, even though it put significant strain on his already fragile health.

Fast forward to April 2025, when the attorney finally reached out regarding an appeal. They sent us paperwork to complete, which we filled out promptly. Once again, we faced months of silence as we tried to determine the hearing date. Eventually, the disability office contacted us the 1st week in July letting us know the hearing will be October. However, on July 24th, we received a letter from the attorney stating, "Please be advised that after final review in preparation for your upcoming hearing, it appears you have been working over the allowable amount; therefore, we will be withdrawing as your representative." I was left in disbelief—who does that? What do they expect people to do.
Very unprofessional. Staff have horrible attitudes. No communication. It's hard to get in touch with people. I would rate 0. Don't hire them it's not worth the hassle.

Please take the time to read the reviews carefully before making your decision. My experience with Jonathan I. Rotstein’s office was not a positive one. Initially, I was only able to speak with a paralegal, despite calling for a consultation. After repeatedly following up, I did eventually speak with Jonathan himself—but unfortunately, the conversation was disappointing. He came across as dismissive and lacking in empathy.

I believe that an attorney should take the time to listen and answer your questions without making you feel rushed or unimportant. That wasn’t the case here. Due to the tone and nature of the interaction, I promptly requested that my daughter’s file be closed.

I’m relieved I made that decision. There are many attorneys out there who are patient, respectful, and genuinely committed to helping their clients.

I wish this firm the best, and if you decide to move forward with them, I sincerely hope your experience is more positive than mine.
~Rosemarie Fletcher ~
*Sr Tax Professional @ RoseMarie Tax Firm LLC

I would not recommend these guys to re-present my worst enemy... these officers of courts did nothing they promised to do during my hearing the guy barely spoke two words which none of that mattered... I know from the start they are not a firm that advocates Zealously for their clients.. they are only about the easy money... they helped my disability case get denied when I have alpha gal syndrome by ADA standards all food allergies especially ones that also ticborne diseases are in fact disabilities this doesn't count the neck and back and ADD and all the other factors that have taken me out ofbmy 25 year career in the electrical industry .. they allowed the system to say I was to young and to smart that I can not work in my career field but just gonna have to go be a copy machine operator job that don't exist... not even gonna go anymore... just k ow they suck and won't help if its anything they gotta do any work for..

My first time being represented by an attorney for SSI leaves much to be desired. Our relationship began March, 2022 whereupon a representative visited my home and interviewed me, leaving in my care a folder of contacts, etc.

It's present day and I thought to my self, here it is my third appointment with the Social Security Determination Services and based on thee correspondence I've received reiterating the appointment, which happens to become a trend with this entity to a redundant level. This letter, in particular, specifically states, and I quote, "Enclosed please find correspondence, which we received from Social Security setting you a doctor's appointment at their expense.".

Well, the location of thee appointment is, according to Google Maps, 50 minutes away from me by car, one hour and fifty minutes by bicycle. I own a moped that is near needing a CVT belt repair, I made mention of this to both the SSDI office and this esteemed attorney office.

I also made mention of just how it would be to attempt that journey from home to appointment and breakdown en route to a calloused response of "we would have to surmise you are non-compliant with our policies and determine your case on the data in our file.
No recourse was offered as in Lyft, taxi etc.,

I thought again to request a 'progress report' from thee day we commenced this relationship to date to receive from my paralegal, who made several attempts to speak as I was speaking, obviously unfamiliar with the art of conversation  ... if two people in a discussion try to speak at the same time,  who's listening???

In any case,  she responded,  "I don't know what you mean ".
Needless to say I was compelled to spell it out that I want what Rothstien and Shiffman has accomplished to date.  I believe a change is in order.
This representative has thee propensity to keep addressing me as "sweetie," which CAN be most annoying,  not to me, though I can adapt that way.
As of July 9, 2024, I requested a letter of separation/severance as they've done nothing since this fiasco commenced. 7/13/24 I have the severance letter and noticed they expect a compensatory fee? For What???  We will see.

That's a lot of 1 star reviews. I wish I could say my experience was better. I would like to direct you to two lawyers who were more helpful. A Richard Zaldivar and Rubenstein Law. I'd also recommend Fulgencio Law😉

Always do your research concerning the experience level of an attorney in your specific case as it relates to Social Security. Not all attorneys are equally knowledgeable. I’m usually better with my research, but I dropped the ball on this one. Now looking further into this firm, they have an awful lot of complaints and are not accredited with the BBB.

I had previously worked with this attorney on my daughter’s Social Security case. While they were somewhat helpful years ago in my case, they didn’t seem particularly knowledgeable or proactive when it came to my daughter’s situation. Over the span of a year, I had minimal contact with them, and I was often the one doing the legwork and sending in documentation.

When I recently found another attorney who is more experienced with my daughter’s specific situation, this office refused to waive their fee rights — despite the fact that they came in after the initial filing and haven’t meaningfully contributed to the reconsideration process. I requested an itemized list of services performed, and to date, I have not received one.

What’s most frustrating is that their unwillingness to release their claim to fees is now preventing me from securing better legal help for my daughter. They showed little interest in understanding or addressing the complexities of her case — especially after I explained that her disability diagnosis came after the age of 18, which led to SSA’s rejection.

I’m left with no choice but to file a formal complaint and seek other avenues to help my daughter. This experience has been deeply disappointing and unnecessarily stressful.

PERSONAL INJURY

1st off, their communication sucks and they never return a call back/ message.  I ve been waiting for my medical insurance company to to contact my law team, in order for me to get paid crumbs, for approximately 2 months and counting.  So far I lost part of my eye site,  $7000. worth of jewelry,  etc. waitingonmy check just to find out that the amount i settled for isn't what I'm getting. I'm pretty sure that after this review I'll be homeless before I getting my settlement $ which was settled almost 2 months ago however, the truth need to be told. I'm never rude, just straight and honest... and attribute that's scarce in the business world now-a-days.
Oh and by the way, if your ever attacked by a disgruntled employee at work, Do not attempt to protect yourself or its basically your fault. It's a lot that I feel about my whole experience but what's the use... it's been like this for some of us for generations. Thx lawyers for your help... NOT!
Thanks for such a beautiful life here in America full of equality and justice for all. LOL

I will co tinue to seek help to secure "my" benefits. Save your time and don't secure a firm that operates based on emotions.

I was struggling with trying to pay my bills and put food on my table, I lost the place I was living in and had to sell my truck to buy a camper. All because I was injured at work at no fault of my own. My doctors appointments were dragged on, and I was being under payed by my employers insurance company... I finally reached out and found Rotstein Shiffman & Broderick. I cannot even put into words how grateful I am for this team of outstanding attorneys. They are a blessing..

Hired them to help with my disability case. They did nothing to help and I ended up getting nothing but b/s from the lawyer who lost the case and now I'm homeless and still seeking help while actually disabled and getting no help.

I had about the same experience as 'Jennifer Coppolino' below where the "lawyer" was NEVER interested in speaking to me or even knowing about my case. This became clear the day of the call with the Judge.

These guys are not good, and the one who only spoke to me 1 time in a year before the call with the Judge was VERY rude.

The entire time I was seeking disability the first go around they said they would help my case and I trusted them... If I could go back in time I would not have used them as I feel without them I had a chance of winning my case because I am honestly disabled. These folks did nothing for me.

They had me speak to the assistant and from the point they said they would take my 'SSDI' case but neither lawyer spoke to me for over a year long.

Everytime I tried to call to speak to them it was blocked and I was told to speak to the assistant. Last couple of months she had me speak to HER assistant, and well I waited until a week before the call with the Judge to finally get to speak to the lawyer who was supposed to represent me!

He called to prep me for the call with the Judge, and he was abusive, and dismissive of me as soon as I tried to tell him I had sent in more updated information on my case including footage, and not even 1 minute in he stops me very rudely and tells me "not to speak when not spoken to and only answer questions that I was rambling on."

Mind you I had not spoken to this man before and he was my 'lawyer' ... This left me ME in shock as I said to him that "I'm only trying to explain to you sir the latest on my health since we have never spoken so you know where I am at and we can be on the same page!"

He told me to be quiet, and also be silent when the Judge talks to me and only answer directly fast, and shut up. Because he felt I would ramble on.

As you can imagine I was floored by his bad attitude, and still tried to be polite and I asked him had he seen the email I sent 2 weeks before showing new footage of my legs and he said no.

Again shocked I asked how come I sent it to his assistant and HER assistant. The guy asked and sure enough he over looked the email with the footage and he saw the footage his words were "Oh my god your legs still look like this?" Showing me he had no idea who I was or my case for about a year.

He said it didn't matter now since he didn't think he had enough time to send it to the JUDGE NOW!!! Mind you I got it to his office as requested A HEAD of time, and this is his answer. He than tells ma again to only answer the Judge in direct fast answers.

Also drops the news that my meeting for my disability with the Judge would be a normal call not in person or even video like Skype. The Judge was also very rude, and dismissive and because of this they failed to help me, and I'm homeless as the person who I was staying with my cousin is now passed away and, and my health is so bad I think I'm dying myself.

Because these people wasted my time with not doing their job that I can't afford to even buy the medication I need to help my health. I had to start my whole case over again and now a YEAR later I'm still waiting and broke, and all thanks to these lawyers who did nothing for me.

The assistants were very nice, and polite even tho last few months I was being passed by the main assistant who sounded over worked, and this is why she had her assistant now deal with me, and she was hard to get a hold of also! So 1 lawyer sent me to his assistant, and than she had her assistant which is now assistant number 2 help or take my calls, and she was also almost never around.

I don't know about other cases but I wouldn't use them in a SSDI case. Not if like myself you need serious help because you're seriously disabled.

We thought it would be a simple case that his public defender could handle but after the PD had several negotiations with the prosecutor, we felt we were out of options, aside from my son admitting to the burglary charge with 3yrs of probation. I spoke with Mr. Ruddy Smith from this firm and he thought we were in a tough spot but said he would do his best. This guy is down to earth with a great deal of experience and very well connected.  He got the charges dropped to a withhold misdemeanor trespass and no probation.  Absolutely amazing.  Ruddy comes with my highest recommendation.

Choosing the right legal representation can be one of the most critical decisions a person makes during difficult, life-altering moments. My husband was in need of an attorney; I searched for a firm that not only possessed the highest level of professional expertise but also treated their clients with genuine empathy and compassion. After our experience with Aaron Delgado & Associates, I can say with confidence that this firm sets the gold standard for what legal advocacy should be.

Throughout the course of my husband's case, the communication from Aaron Delgado & Associates was exemplary. Every question we had was answered promptly and in terms we could understand. Legal jargon was broken down into accessible language, ensuring he was always aware of his options and the status of his case.
Moreover, the team kept us informed about important developments, deadlines, and next steps. Even when the news was difficult, they delivered it with honesty and compassion. This transparency fostered a sense of security and partnership; I truly felt that we were working together towards the best possible outcome.

Of course, compassion and professionalism must be matched by results. In this regard, Aaron Delgado & Associates excelled. Their mastery of the law was evident in every aspect of my case. They meticulously reviewed evidence, anticipated challenges, and crafted a strategy tailored specifically to my circumstances.
Their preparation and attention to detail were nothing short of impressive. In the courtroom, their poise and command were clear, and their advocacy on my behalf was passionate yet composed. Ultimately, the outcome of my case exceeded my expectations, and I attribute this success directly to the expertise and diligence of Aaron Delgado & Associates.
NOT GUILTY!!

I am  forever grateful to Attorney Aaron and John  for fighting so hard and proving my dad’s innocence. reviews.text: Everything in this review is public record or my opinion of Attorney Harr. No payment was ever dropped off. This entire case has been nothing but delays and lies by Attorney Harr. It seems he likes to intimidate and harass, refuses to read or review discovery requests, communicate, & follow proper courtroom decorum. He has filed 3 frivolous lawsuits against me all of his complaints were riddled with lies. He accused me of being a criminal, I've never been charged with a crime. Attorney Harr lost in court that day, on July 15 against me, a Pro Se defendant. You can look the case up, 2024 13413 CICI. I was referred to Chanfrau and Chanfrau,  From the moment I walked through the door, the staff were very outgoing and polite.
I met with attorney Greg Olsen, and the senior assistant Roxie Courson. They took the time to listen to my story and guided me through what the steps were and answered all my questions. Once we agreed that they were gonna take my case, they took control of taking care of my insurance company the other person‘s insurance company the doctors all the bills and everything else was handled through them.

When all was said and done in the end, Roxie went as far to fight with the doctors offices to get the bills reduced, and actually managed to get me a settlement of more than what I even came close to expect.
I will say that Chanfrau and Chanfrau has a great team, very professional and always available to assist you no matter how big or small the question or issue was that had me concerned. Roxie was a real superstar and on top of everything even though she was going through some personal family issues of her own she managed to perform outstandingly for my settlement. For any public adjuster needing a reliable legal partner, or for property owners facing tough claims, I highly recommend Michael Ciocchetti, PLLC. We are facing a mountain of situations right now that we need help and support with and the frustrations keep building on a daily basis. Again, I can’t apologize enough and would like to try to make amends."

Based on the volume of documentation you intended to discuss, you were advised that a one-hour consultation would be necessary, at our standard rate of $330. reviews.ownerResponse: We have reached out to you via phone as well as email regarding this miscommunication. Before you all paid the consult fee, it was explained in detail - as we advise our consults with legal details and information, and may even offer referrals if the case is not a right fit for our firm. Additionally, we make it clear to potential clients at the time of scheduling a consult, that documents and details will not be reviewed prior. Our goal is to ensure cases get the right attorney at the right time for the right situation, of course. As explained, we did apologize for unexpectedly running late and it could not be helped. We asked you at that time if you wanted to reschedule and you noted it was just fine. And, regarding phone usage during our meetings - our hope is that our clients understand that we want to ensure we are not missing any potential emergencies - with either family or clients. These tools also allow us to look up specific questions/issues potential clients may have at the time of the meeting. At the meeting's conclusion, you all seemed pleased and said you were considering engaging with us, so we are taken quite by surprise at this review. We do look forward to hearing back from you, and feel it's important to let service providers know of satisfaction during the meeting, to give small local businesses the chance to make things right and address any potential issues. reviews.text: My wife and I began the process of a residency application for my mother and father in-laws back in 2014. After our attorney unexpectedly retired, we were left without any outcome at all. So, we hired a second law firm to complete the application process for my mother and father in-law. This turned out to be a very frustrating process. Though the application for my mother in-law was successful, the application for my father in-law was denied, his case being much more complicated. We were unable to establish any communication with this law firm, and it became apparent they were not competent to take my father in-laws case to the next level. We almost accepted that we would never be able to get a residency card for my father-in-law.  However, my business attorney, who is excellent in taking care of my company's legal matters did some research for me to find a good immigration attorney. Here is what he recommended in his words written in an email to me. William Pincus wrote "I don’t really know the immigration attorneys.  But I did some research, and this guy seems like a standout. David F. Vedder, He is based in Central Florida but handles cases world-wide. He is AV-Rated (which, of all the lawyer “honors” floating around, is the only one that is truly earned and not just a popularity contest) and Board Certified in Immigration & Naturalization. This turned out to be Excellent advice. As it turned out, with brilliant legal maneuvers (in my opinion) successful, as my father in-law now has his residency and lives with us in South Florida. The legal staff were excellent, communication superb and the outcome exceeded my expectations, and I might add at a very reasonable cost. I cannot thank David Vedder, Jacqueline Benitez - Practice Manager, Lydia Jung - Associate Attorney, and Christy Ackerman- Sr. Legal Assistant enough. I have recommended them to at least two other friends, one of which has a case with them. If you want the best look no further, this is the team you need. Sounds like an advertisement slogan but it is the truth. Patrick J Forshee.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your heartfelt and detailed review. We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your journey and the challenges you faced before finding our team. It means a great deal to us that we were able to help your family through such a complex situation and ultimately achieve a successful outcome for your father-in-law.

Your kind words about our legal strategy, communication, and support team are deeply appreciated. We work hard to ensure every client feels informed, supported, and confident throughout their case, and it's incredibly rewarding to hear that reflected in your experience.

We’re also honored that you’ve recommended us to others — there’s no greater compliment. Thank you again for placing your trust in us. We’re thrilled to have played a part in reuniting your family, and we wish you all the very best moving forward

I’ve worked with five different immigration lawyers from across the United States, and without a doubt, Mr. Vedder has been the absolute best. After a long and complex journey, my case was finally approved and I received my permanent residency — and it’s all thanks to their hard work and commitment.

Compared to the other lawyers I’ve worked with, this law office stands in a league of its own. They genuinely cared about my case and did everything in their power to help me. I’m beyond grateful for everything they’ve done over the years. I couldn’t have asked for better representation. reviews.ownerResponse:
Frank,
Thank you so much for your incredibly kind and thoughtful words. It’s been an absolute honor to walk this journey with you over the past five years, and we’re truly thrilled to see it end with such a successful outcome.

Your perseverance and trust throughout the process played a big role in that success, and we’re grateful to have been part of your story. On behalf of myself, Veronica, Christy, and the entire team — thank you for allowing us to represent you. Your appreciation means the world to us.

Congratulations on receiving your permanent residency — we’re so happy for you and wish you all the best in this new chapter!

Thank you for your review! We're glad you had a positive experience with our firm and truly appreciate your feedback.

If there's anything we could have done to make your experience a 5-star one, we’d love to hear from you. We're always looking to improve and better serve our clients.

Thanks again for choosing us — and please don’t hesitate to reach out if we can assist you in the future.

reviews.text: I sought the assistance of David F. Vedder PA with assistance regarding my in laws green card process. Their case was extremely complex as they are Russian nationals residing in Russia without access to western COVID vaccines, the obtainment of which was a requirement for most immigration cases. Mr. Yi Lee was my attorney and navigated the case expertly the process, which required had added complexity due to no consulates being open in Russia during that time. Mr. Lee insured that the COVID vaccine requirement could be waived because it was unavailable in Russia.  Mr Lee and the entire team we prompt to respond to questions and provided great customer service during a very exciting and potentially stressful process. My in laws were able to arrive in the USA and obtain their green cards, and it has been life changing process for all of us, and we really appreciate the great team at DFVPA who assisted us through the entire process.

reviews.text: Well it’s difficult to find where should I start when drafting this review, after 8 years struggling with our green card application and after a RFE and a I-130 denial. I have to say we have a complicated case and a very difficult situation when we were referred to Attorney David Vedder firm in early 2022, before talking with attorney Vedder and Yi , I have consulted over a dozen immigration lawyers across US which they all have great ratings and reputation, most of them rejected my case on spot and very few considered we can try but they don’t see too much hope in our case. It’s the discussion with Vedder firm that they truly understood and believed us and most importantly they were willing to fight with us. Even we understood it’s very rare of our case approved but faith was all I needed in 2022. It took a good 3 months to prepare a 400 pages RFE to USCIS 2022, it took another 2 months to prepare a appeal motion after USCIS’s denial in 2023 and it’s another 15 months waiting period till we finally received USCIS’s approval on 6/3/2024! This is a life changing news to my family and Vedder firm has made this happen! Along the 2 years journey working with Vedder firm, they engage, respond quick and always be there when we need guidance. I say, if you have immigration issues or need help, call attorney Vedder and you will be taken care of.

reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Hardware, we are unable to identify the client you are referring to, however we take all feedback seriously and are committed to providing the best service to our clients. It is important to clarify that paralegals cannot represent anyone at trial, and only attorneys are authorized to do so. Mr. Vedder has never and will never send a paralegal to represent anyone at trial; furthermore, a judge would never allow it. We always aim to act in the best interest of our clients and regret that this was not the experience in this case.  We are glad to hear that your fiancé has found a lawyer who appears to be a better fit. We wish you both the best for the future. So, I am a very new client. I did my due diligence prior to contacting Hill & Ponton. I considered a number of criteria, but the impressively high success rating, their obviously thorough vetting process and, in my case, I was happy to see they are in my local vacinity. I don't forsee that really coming into play, but it's nice to know.
I can't yet speak to my personal results in terms of increasing my rating, but I can say that so far, I have had prompt and considerate communication, Professionalism and clear, simple, easy to understand terms.
I see some reviews complaining about "high costs" As many of you know, the VA claims process is daunting. To have such a reputable company handle everything for you and guarantee results is almost invaluable.
So you only pay 30% of any back pay plus expenditure reimbursment. You end up with roughly 70% of your back pay. That is likely to be a significant amount. In my opinion, that is almost a bonus. From that point on, you receive the fair, tax free and significant funds you desererve for the rest of your life along with myriad benefits including the possibility of never paying property tax ever again depending on the state you live in.
Maybe it's better to look at the big picture.

In the begining with them it was fantastic, very communitive had a personal doctor do my diagnosis for my symptoms and was able to answer all my questions. As time went on throughout the years I did notice this communication start to fade and it became very hard to get a hold of them or hear back within a reasonable timeframe. The fee is high as well at 30% That's my only criticism though. I would recommend using them if your stuck at a lower rate and don't know what your doing with the VA.

You cannot reach a human being at this firm.  They go to lunch from 2 to 3 and never get back to you. How can you call that client service???  Terrible
These guys take care of veterans at any level of claim. Call them today!

I would like to thank Stacey Clark and her amazing legal team at Hill and Ponton. In just under 15 months I went from 90% to 100% Va Disability rating. They fought hard for me and my family. They kept me informed on every level of the appeal process. If you need someone to help you out please get a free consultation ASAP. Sincerely, Michael Pelezo Middleburg Florida

As a lawyer and a veteran, I highly recommend Hill & Ponton to any veteran seeking an increase in their VA Disability claim. They are experts with a highly competent, responsive, efficient staff that expeditiously handle VA Claims. They will appeal unfair VA decisions and, in doing so, achieve desired results. Dealing with local or state VA agencies is a waste of time. If you want results within a reasonable time, you need Hill & Ponton.

One popular VA firm in Rhode Island denied taking my VA Claim outright, but Hill & Ponton took it and was successful in taking me from 50% to 100% with retroactive back pay. From intake through settlement, I was impressed and appreciated H&P's well-organized, tech-savvy administrative staff's operational efficiency combined with electronic data security capabilities.

Its staff was so highly efficient with its paperwork that I didn't have to communicate directly with any attorneys. With 49 years in the legal profession, I know that this is a hallmark of a top-notch, highly organized, premier law firm. The attorneys give orders, legal strategy, and direction to its administrative staff, and the staff at H&P followed up expertly, efficiently, and expeditiously.

The most difficult,  disgruntled, and entitled clients are the ones who ignorantly demand unnecessary, direct communication with their attorney. Additionally, I've worked in personal injury law firms and discovered that Hill & Ponton is compassionate, caring, and dedicated to fighting and winning battles for their veteran clients. If you watch their YouTube videos, you will be convinced of their compassion, caring, their thorough and up-to-date  knowledge of veteran's concerns and issues during the Q&A segment. They educate veterans on all issues with their YouTube videos, books, and pamphlets. For several years, the VA stalled and threw my 50% claim in the trash, Hill & Ponton recycled my claim from the VA's dumpster and won a miraculous 100% victory in less than two years! I've worked for top-rated A-1 international law firms, but in the highly specialized area of VA Claims, you will need a top-rated A-1 law firm: Hill & Ponton.

Mathew Hill and Stephanie from this law firm won EVERY  appeal case for me and was personal, kind and euruditious. I would like to recommend them to ALL veterans in need respectfully..
Donald Skelton

For over 10 years, I tried to file my VA disability claim on my own because the VA was treating me for Service Connected Disabilities but, would not give me the ratings. I needed help. Several VA Law Firms later, based on nothing more than a 'feel good interaction with the Hill and Ponton Team', again, I sent out my VA File. Within a few days, Carol Ponton personally called to except my case and verify the findings within the VA File. Now, I was part of their Team which included; Carol Ponton, Brenda Duplantis, Amanda Dejesas, Amanda Martin, and others. I was totally impressed with the Team's knowledge of VA law, VA procedures, and VA process efficiencies that flat out work. Through out the process, a member of the Team would request a step which, generally because of my VA disabilities I did not complete. Without fail, a Team member would reach out with a call or email to check on me. Looking back, I cannot emphasize how important that became.  Each Team member of Hill and Ponton always responded with questions, corrections, and updates by phones calls or emails in a timely manner with compassion and understanding for this challenged veteran. We military veterans are fully trained to understand and execute our duties along each step of our assigned processes and responsibilities to achieve a successful mission. After over 10 years disappointment, this Team successfully completed the mission only because the entire Hill and Ponton Team went above and beyond to help this challenged veteran and family. Be advised, reach out to them.

The Hill & Ponton team are a blessing!  Like many veterans frustrated with claiming service-connected injuries with the VA; I turned to Hill & Ponton!  Attorney Matthew Hill is a first-class professional that took the time to understand my service and the lasting injuries from it, filed on my behalf, and we won!  Forever grateful to this wonderful team representing our injured veterans!

Hill & Ponton pursued my case with professionalism and tenacity. They listened to me compassionately then took action ferociously. They had my disability rating increased from 70% to 100%. Because of their efforts, I can now provide for my family again.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for trusting us and allowing us to help you with your appeal process.

I have lived with my disability since 1967. I filed with the VA in 2019, as the separation interview  never mentioned anything about the VA. I was given 60% and thought that’s all I would get. I contacted Hill & Ponton after my rejection for increased benefits. They told me that they thought they could help me. They did all of the work and now I have total and permanent disability status. They are the best. I could never thank them enough. Thank you Hill and Ponton staff for your dedication.

They have a good reputation with the veteran community. I have some ongoing claims so I will know more later.
For now they are professional and responsive.

What a blessing. Hill & Ponton has, hands down, the most professional, knowledgeable, and kind staff we've had the pleasure to work with. They genuinely root for vets, and do not treat them as just a number. Before a friend led us to this firm, we had horrible experience after horrible experience. I can wholeheartedly say Hill & Ponton will work tirelessly to help veterans and families of veterans receive the benefits they deserve for bravely serving this country.
Complete waste of time trying to work with them. Good YouTube videos, but very poor delivery of the exact services they claim to be experts in.

I really appreciated the assistance I received from Hill & Ponton Lawyers. Their entire staff was courteous, professional and very understanding. I was at my breaking point of dealing with Veterans Affairs for the past 10 years getting nowhere. I have serious service connected disabilities; however, the VA would not acknowledge my claims past 80%, even though the C & P evaluators presented enough evidence to show I was unable to work and was suffering with my disabilities.  Hill & Ponton took up my case and in six months they got me approved for P & T disability. I can't thank their staff enough because it has been a long rough battle on my own and I would not have broken through to 100%.  I personally want to thank Sara, Danielle, and the rest of the courteous staff that took my calls and always kept me abreast of what was the next thing to do and expect. Just the emotional strain that is lifted is enough to thank them for.  Totally professional and caring group of people which is a rarity today.  Again my heartfelt thanks to Hill & Ponton.

After talking with Sara K. Hill, she immediately went into action. Hill and Ponton is a well-oiled machine. They know the process inside and out. Out of their own pocket, they paid for a service-veteran doctor who evaluated me over a zoom meeting. They had all the VA forms necessary for my case and represented me as if I was their only client. Because of their hard work, the VA gave me 30% disability. That was not acceptable to Sara! After a year of petitioning the VA, they paid once again out of their own pocket to have that same doctor re-evaluate me again. The assessment was given to the VA. The VA concluded that based on these evaluations, they gave me 100% Disability! Because I started this process ten years prior to Hill and Ponton taking my case (as my last hope), the VA back-paid me $270,000. Hill and Ponton, hands down, is THE best out there!
Excellent working with them on my ssdi case and we won the case thanks again for all the help from Houston TX

The time I spent with Attny Shelly Mark and Mary filing my SSD paperwork and all my Drs. Appts, etc, I know I couldn’t have done this alone. Mary was also available at almost every call I made or responded to every email quickly.

Even in the midst of this pandemic, they never forgot me. All my SS Drs. appointments were canceled and I felt I was just out here floundering not knowing but Mary always called me to let me know they were doing all they could to keep my case on track. About 2 weeks later I got a call from her stating that my next appt would be virtual and if necessary, future appts and hearings would be virtual as long as the pandemic should last.

Sherry was there through the process of the hearing, speaking to the judge and the vocational rehab person on my behalf. I left the hearing feeling really well as to how the hearing went. Within the month I received notification that I did win my disability case!

I could not have done this with out Sherry and Mary. There is no doubt, I was in no way mentally prepared to fill out all the paper.
Thank you Mary and Sherry!
The best, thank God.

reviews.text:
Ended up getting my disability recognized on my own, but I originally approached them for help. I asked for them to be transparent on costs, knowing full well that they would get a portion. They said that they would collect more than legally allowed, and when I asked about that, they had a tantrum and cut off communication. I tried a second time a couple of years later, and since I had a higher disability rating than the first time, they wouldn't even talk to me.
Simply put, they want your money. They want easy cases they can do the bare minimum on to get maximum benefit for themselves. This would be fine if they only had a passing interest in representing veterana who need help. They don't.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi D, That is wonderful news that you received the benefits that you deserve! I am very sorry to hear that you had not one, but two unexpected outcomes with our firm.
We do our best not to overwhelm our lawyers with too many cases so we can ensure that all veterans getting represented receive expert legal service. But, that means we cannot take on every case we would like to. We really do want to help everyone so we will either refer you to another law firm or provide you with some direction on what you may be able to do next. We’ve invested significant time and effort into our Written and Video blogs on our website to be the best free resources for any veteran to use as they apply and appeal their claims, and I hope those resources were helpful as you went through the process. Again, we are happy to hear that you are now receiving benefits!
All the best,
-CC
Thank you Edwina!

reviews.text:
I was genuinely hopeful when my godson was appointed a guardian during his custody case. I believed it would finally give him the opportunity to share his perspective with the court. At the time, he was around 16 years old. Unfortunately, over the course of nearly two years, Mr. Ellzey has spent more time and would  rather listen to his father's side instead of actually being there for my Godson

He was suppose to be his voice in court and he was let down numerous times.

Despite my godson clearly expressing to Mr. Ellzey that he felt unsafe with his father. Mr. Ellzey brushed it off repeatedly. These matters ended up being dismissed in court.

At no point did Mr. Ellzey appear to take my godson’s concerns seriously, nor did he act in his best interests or serve as his advocate. —this entire experience was deeply disappointing and I wish no other child to have to go through this.

I was actually really excited when my son was appointed a guardian for his custody case. I thought he would finally have the chance to give his input to the court. My son was about 16 at this time. Mr. Ellzey spent about 5 hours total with my son in almost 2 years. He spent over 25 hours with his father, though.... my son told Mr. Ellzey he was unsafe with his father. Mr. Ellzey laughed about it in court and said my son was just strong willed. My son wrote Mr. Ellzey a letter outlining all of the crimes his father had committed... Mr. Ellzey laughed about it in court and said he should become a lawyer. Never once did Mr. Ellzey take anything my son was saying seriously and he definitely had no concern for my son's interests or being my son's voice. Add to that insanely high hourly fees and allowing his father to stack up charges that I had to pay half of just to keep talking and meeting for no reason and this was an extremely disappointing experience.

I cannot say enough great things about everyone at Buckmaster and Ellzey. I struggled with my divorce for 2 years and was let down by other attorneys previously.

Everyone here listen to my situation and worked with me to get everything finally resolved. They went above and beyond to make sure that I had the assistance that I needed to finalize everything.

I highly recommend them and wish I had found them sooner.

It was a scary day indeed when a relative of ours relayed to us the trouble he had gotten himself into.  He had always been a good caring soul who, it appeared, had been taken advantage of.

A detective was now wanting to interview our relative and we knew we needed some professional help and FAST.  Through a friend we found Attorney Buckmaster.

The moment we met him, a sense of peace came upon us.  He was such a good listener and laid out the good, the bad and the ugly of how things could go.

It was now a waiting game between Attorney Buckmaster and the Sheriff’s office.  Within 8 days, the situation was resolved and we all breathed a sigh of relief.

We knew going in this would not be easy for Attorney Buckmaster, but we trusted him and knew he would do his very best.  His staff which included Laney set our minds at ease with any questions we had, and she sent out her Friday summary as she said she always does for clients.

We will forever be grateful to Attorney Buckmaster and his staff.

My family had the distinct pleasure of working with David Ellzey as our attorney, and I am compelled to share our exceptional experience with others who might be seeking legal representation that not only delivers results but also cares deeply about its clients.

From our very first interaction, David established himself as a paragon of professionalism. His demeanor is always composed and reassuring, which significantly alleviates the stress that legal challenges inevitably bring. David’s professionalism is matched by his extraordinary legal skills. He possesses a profound understanding of the law, which he leverages to craft strategic solutions that are tailored to meet specific needs.

David’s support throughout the legal process was unwavering. He was always accessible, promptly responding to emails and phone calls, which is something we deeply appreciated. It was evident that he prioritizes his clients’ needs and schedules, always making time to address our questions and concerns.

One of David’s most commendable qualities is his ability to explain legal jargon and complex proceedings in a way that is easy to understand. This clarity was invaluable as it empowered us to make informed decisions about our daughter's case. David ensured that we were not just spectators but active participants in the legal process. His detailed explanations provided comfort and a clear understanding of each step of the case.

Moreover, David’s compassion sets him apart. He approached our case not just with legal acumen but with genuine empathy and understanding. His concern for our daughter's welfare transcended the confines of professional obligation; he truly cared about her outcome and well-being. This level of empathy and dedication is rare and distinguished him as not just a remarkable attorney but also a supportive ally in our challenging time.

If you are looking for an attorney who embodies professionalism, expert knowledge, clear communication, and genuine compassion, David Ellzey is the ideal choice. He not only met but exceeded our expectations, and we are profoundly grateful for his outstanding service. We wholeheartedly recommend David Ellzey to anyone in need of legal assistance, as he will handle your case with the utmost skill and care.

Mr. Ellzey did an amazing job fighting for me, and giving me the resources I needed to fight for my parental rights with my daughter.  I am so pleased with the outcome, and I would highly recommend them to help other fathers who are in the same position.

reviews.text:
My wife and I wanted to make sure all that we had worked for would go to our daughter without any problems.  We found Debra Simms from a friend and set up a meeting.

I had some idea of what legal documents we needed.  I had no idea what was necessary for a smooth transition for our daughter if we passed away unexpectedly.

Debra Simms explained and listed what was necessary to make it easy for our daughter.  She provided printed docs that were easy to comprehend  and then emailed us a copy of  all the legal papers we needed.  We checked them for names, dates, etc before meeting for the signing and notarizing of more documents that we had ever seen.

We were very happy with the lawyer we had chosen.  A very nice lady.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for this wonderful review. It means a great deal to me to have satisfied clients. I enjoyed working with you and your wife very much.
Debra

reviews.text:
I am writing to make a formal complaint against Debra Simms. She was hired to excute a Will and Trust for my aunt and showed no compassion, empathy or interest on her dying days.
We reached out several times to Ms Simms for help  when my aunt quickly declined in her health .
We were expecting at a minimum for her to guide us through this process only to realize that was not her interest or priority. She refused to go out of her way in any manner to make this a smooth transition. Her demeanor in speaking with me was belittling,  egotistical, and indifferent of my aunts needs.
I am writing this in the hopes that no one else has to experience a crisis like ours with a heartless attorney.

.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dana,
I am taken aback by your review, since I had made numerous efforts to accommodate the needs of your aunt while her health was in rapid decline.
Respectfully, your aunt came to me over a month before her passing. Learning of her prognosis, I prepared her estate plan on a rush basis and promptly forwarded the drafts for her review. During the weeks thereafter, there were continued discussions about the final version of the documents.
After the documents were finally approved, I made numerous attempts to arrange for a time for your aunt to sign the documents, including a time convenient for you and your work schedule, so that you could bring your aunt to my office. I even arranged for my staff to come into the office on their days off, while I was on vacation, to accomplish the signing. However, you declined that appointment.
The very day that I returned from vacation, we made another attempt to schedule a signing, but unfortunately, you told me that your aunt was no longer lucid nor responsive to communication.
It was in sadness that I learned of your aunt’s passing the very next day, as I had grown very fond of her during our time working together.
Debra
Thank you so much, Ms. Walsh!  I appreciate it.

In all honesty, words cannot express our appreciation and gratitude to the folks at the Law Offices of Debra Simms.  The profound professionalism and sincere compassion that was shown to us in every step of the process is something that you rarely see these days.  We had no idea what to expect when making the initial phone call after the passing of my mother in-law but knew immediately that she had made to right decision when choosing this firm, making sure that her final wishes would be met.  I would like to personally thank Legal Assistant, Ms. Kendall Lawrence for her patience and compassion from day one when my husband came to the office for the first appointment, literally toting behind him a carry-on suitcase full of paperwork.  She explained in detail what we would need to do in a manner that we understood without a lot of legal mumbo jumbo.  She is a true asset to this firm and we will always be grateful to her, thank you Kendall and thank you Attorney Simms.

reviews.text: This is not easy for me as I prefer to write positive reviews, but I felt this had to be done! I went to Cobb Cole in Deland because they were recomended. I had been through a very rough time after a tornado hit my house a few years ago. The people that were hired to restore my home basically took the insurance money and all I got was a new roof. The restoration group I hired left me high and dry with many things to complete. My Master bathroom was demolished and left that way. What little work that was done, I paid for all the materials, even though the company was paid in insurance money. I went to Cobb Cole and explained the situation and they even said they were aware of this company as they had another client that was going through the same thing. After a year with very sparse comunication from Cobb and Cole, they finally said they talked to the man we had dealt with from the restoration company and basically, I felt like they were on his side. All of a sudden the fees that had been in the $150-$175 range were suddenly increased and they even brought in another attorney just to tell us that there was nothing we could do. We were told if someone demolishes your bathroom, they are entilted to be compensated to fix it. Since the insurance company would not agree to it, we were left without a bathroom. Three years and 1,395 dollars spent to the law firm and I still do not have a bathroom. It is very funny how they acted like they could help us, untill they took what little money we had. I am pretty sure this was not the first case like this they ever had, so why take advantage of older people like us. Why not just say the first day we went in there that they would not be able to help us. I would not recomend this law firm to anyone. I used the Deland group so I can't speak about the rest. I really thought they were kind in the beginning, now I see that they looked at us the same way the unscrupulous restoration company did. I will say that they hit us with a large bill in the end that had us looking at getting a loan to pay them off. We started paying off the bill as we could and then they hit us with 20% interest. We called to get a final pay off amount and we were told that they would use the retainer as the final pay off. I am very grateful for that act of kindness.

I have used Cobb Cole for many years for several different matters and areas of law.  The entire team is professional and work in a timely manner.  When dealing with estate planning, an area that I am not familiar with, no question is a ignorant one.  I always feel comfortable and know that they will handle each matter in the best way possible.  They also represent my local business and real estate law transactions.  Kuddos to being the areas largest law firms in our area for over 95 years!
Attorney Sydney Cichon is caring and compassionate in all that she does.  I would recommend her and Cobb Cole anyday to my friends and family.

I cannot recommend them enough! Thank you so much!!!

Edit: please read the real reviews for this firm! It seems as though someone has been making fake accounts to leave multiple derogatory reviews. My personal experience was very pleasant!!!

Although the divorce was settled it by mediation, I felt a sense of assurance that if we went to trial, he would have performed very well in trial.   The office was organized and responsive. Ms. Danielle Latona, in particular, handled the administration; scheduling, information collation, discovery binders and emails. During mediation, we were able to pull from the binders easily and timely due to her diligence.  I wholeheartedly and unreservedly recommend The Law Office of Jeffrey A. Klein.
